title: Wikipedias biased Evangelion section
wikitext:
[]: Wikipedia's biased Evangelion section             Quote Post by ninetyninenails » Thu Oct 19, 2006 1:56 pm Well I was borwsing Wikipedia for fun and noticed how bias some of the things on there seem to be. For instance it says that Rei is his mom/sister but then under Rei's relationship section it talks about some of the romantic like scenes that involve the two. Though I am a Shinji/Rei fan I am also a Shinji/Asuka fan and it also has bias information on them like how he is sexually attracted to her, but isn't he sexually attracted to all the other girls? It lists other bias things but I don't feel like typing it all so have a look for yourself. [url]http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rei_Ayanami[/url] [url]http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shinji_Ikari[/url] [url]http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Asuka_Langley_Soryu[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Oct 19, 2006 4:51 pm I don't think the Eva Wikipedia articles represent any online community consensus. I believe Reichu did some editing at one point, but other than that, I don't think there is any forum input into those articles. In any event, I don' think there should be any attempt to explain Evangelion in the Wikipedia articles. They should basically consist of "Evangelion is very open to interpretation." Or words to that effect. While we're on the topic, it's been said before, but this community could do with a wiki of its own, if only for archival purposes. Actually, was I supposed to be looking into that?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Oct 19, 2006 4:52 pm Don't bother trying to correct it in the effort of fixing it. I attempted to fix a number of blatant errors, and was cited for vandalism. Like, this for example: She lives by herself in a cluttered and nearly barren apartment in Tokyo-3.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Thu Oct 19, 2006 7:36 pm The Wikipedia NGE article and all related shit is horrible. There is some serious editting to be done there, but I'm not the master fanwanker here so it's not up to me to fix it.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Thu Oct 19, 2006 7:54 pm I just saw some idiot bio student from johns hopkins try to nail Newt Gringrich by quoting the wikipedia article about him. WTF

[]:             Quote Post by Eternal Yamcha » Thu Oct 19, 2006 11:11 pm I personally won't dog the Evangelion Wikipedia article, had it not been for that, I'd never have come to this site. Inversely, on the other hand, you guys CAN blame the Evangelion Wikipedia article for bringing me here. The Evangelion Wikipedia article should only be used as a basis to cover the series, not actually attempt to be indepth about it. IT should be acatalog of basic information, episode synopsis and links to Evangelion related sites. Simple as that. If anyone has the time and wants to do it, go check out the TV.com Evangelion section. I did once, for a minute or two. Don't remember much.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Oct 20, 2006 11:16 am Eternal Yamcha wrote: Inversely, on the other hand, you guys CAN blame the Evangelion Wikipedia article for bringing me here.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Fri Oct 20, 2006 1:30 pm Leakypedia has become a haven for mall ninjas and philistines alike. Just recently, somebody deleted a whole bunch of articles that I was using as references for my writing work... and then there's all the vandalism, too. I say we take off, nuke the site from orbit.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Fri Oct 20, 2006 4:46 pm Bitching about wikipedia is not a meme. And everyone knows the people that run wikipedia are dicks, doesn't change the fact that some articles are pretty informative. And some vandalism is pretty funny too.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Fri Oct 20, 2006 11:07 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Don't bother trying to correct it in the effort of fixing it. I attempted to fix a number of blatant errors, and was cited for vandalism. Like, this for example: She lives by herself in a cluttered and nearly barren apartment in Tokyo-3.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Oct 21, 2006 10:03 am Hexon.Arq wrote: Or someone just hates you. They have your IP, no?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Oct 21, 2006 11:55 am Hexon.Arq wrote: Or someone just hates you. They have your IP, no?

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sat Oct 21, 2006 1:21 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Hexon.Arq wrote: Or someone just hates you. They have your IP, no?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Oct 21, 2006 4:12 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: Aren't those IP addresses listed in the page histories for unregistered users in lieu of user names? Or are they just randomly assigned number IDs? Anyway, if you're registered as The Eva Monkey, it would make it a whole lot easier. Are you?

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sat Oct 21, 2006 10:54 pm Well, it's been a day since I changed that sentence you were talking about, and I ain't got whacked on the head yet. In fact, it's still there. I think someone might hate you.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Oct 23, 2006 8:07 pm I had no idea Wiki was that biased and that there Eva info was false. As well as the fact the people are asses when you try to add CORRECT INFORMATION. (I think I've heard it's the same with this Sailormoon-based wiki site.) Though I should have been tipped off by this one line about Kaworu (it seems to be removed by now) that there info is screwed up. The conversation with Seele in 24' revealing Kaworu has the soul of Adam is supposedly a "plot hole" because Kaworu should have known Adam wasn't the one on the cross if he has Adam's soul. Um...right. Someone remind me again what the physical body has to do with the soul? :roll:

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Mon Oct 23, 2006 9:26 pm Another problem with LOL WIKIPEDIA is that it states that Asuka's birthday is December 4, 2001 in her article, but in the Evangelion timeline it states it's December 4, 2000 . Which one is correct? I find 2001 to be doubtful since she's stated to be 14 in the series, and if she was born on 2001 she would be 13 during most of the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Oct 23, 2006 10:09 pm Yeah, Wiki seems to state that Asuka is acutally 13 in the majority of the series and turns 14 towards the end. No idea which one is valid, sorry. :(

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Mon Oct 23, 2006 10:18 pm The CI states that Asuka graduated from college in Germany at age 14, which would indicate, of course, that she is already 14 we meet her in #08.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Oct 24, 2006 2:53 am The 2001 birth date is the most widely cited one. not just Wikipedia's. It's not as if the '00 date would have been ruled out by being pre-2I (as if that made a difference).
